purpose: List, create, reorder, and (TBD) update TB groupings within a financialList. Groups are the actual codes (e.g. 1000 Cash, 1400 Prepaid).

create.body_signal: financialGroupId = INSERT-AFTER ANCHOR (NOT a create/update flag). 0 = only for first group into an EMPTY list; otherwise the PRECEDING group's id.
create.script_function: scripts.groups.create_financial_group(client_id, financial_list_id, index, name, account_type_id, classification_id, headers, anchor_group_id=0)
create.bulk: scripts.groups.build_chain_create_groups_js(client_id, financial_list_id, groups, headers, start_anchor=0) - one browser call, threads the anchor across N creates
create.verification_status: anchor rule live-captured 2026-05-30 (clientId 100173). Sending 0 into a non-empty list -> HTTP 400 'Invalid financial group.'

reorder.body_signal: full group object + positionFinancialGroupId (anchor) + positionId (0=before/above, 1=after/below). financialGroupIds are stable across reorders.
reorder.bulk: scripts.groups.build_reorder_groups_js(client_id, financial_list_id, ordered_groups, headers, sort_by_index=True) - ascending-sort chain, each group PUT after its predecessor

update.body_signal: rename / reclassify / re-index an EXISTING group. Body = the reorder PUT shape (full account-type trio + positionFinancialGroupId + positionId) with the edited index/name/classification.
update.script_function: scripts.groups.update_financial_group(client_id, financial_list_id, financial_group_id, index, name, account_type_id, classification_id, anchor_group_id, headers, position=1)
update.verification_status: LIVE-VERIFIED 2026-05-31 (clientId 100173, list 14737): renamed a group in place via PUT (count unchanged). The old POST-with-own-id is CONFIRMED WRONG — it created a duplicate group. PUT also REQUIRES a position anchor (positionFinancialGroupId+positionId) or 400 'Invalid group location'; to edit without moving, anchor on the group currently directly above the target with position=1.

list_types.purpose: Returns accountType array with nested classifications. Same data bundled at config/group_account_types.json - read that instead of calling the network if you already know the classification name.

request_body_shape_create.name: string (e.g. 'Cash & Equivalents') -- UNIQUE KEY within a list
request_body_shape_create.financialGroupId: int INSERT-AFTER ANCHOR (0 = first into empty list; else preceding group's id)

request_body_shape_reorder_put.name: string
request_body_shape_reorder_put.accountType: string REQUIRED (the type string, e.g. 'Asset') - from GET row's type.acctType. Omitting -> 400 'Invalid account type.'
request_body_shape_reorder_put.accountTypeId: int REQUIRED - from GET row's type.accountTypeId
request_body_shape_reorder_put.accountTypeClassificationId: int REQUIRED - NOT in the GET row; resolve from the classification NAME (group.classification). Omitting -> 400 'Invalid account type classification.'

_reorder_put_field_note: Corrected 2026-05-31: the reorder PUT requires the FULL account-type trio (accountType STRING + accountTypeId + accountTypeClassificationId) - unlike the create POST. The list_financial_groups GET nests accountType/accountTypeId under type{} and gives classification by NAME only, so the int classification id must be resolved. scripts.groups._group_put_type_fields() does this; build_reorder_groups_js() / move_financial_group() pass GET rows straight through.

semantics.anchor_rule: financialGroupId on POST is an insert-after anchor, not a create/update flag. Bulk create must chain: create -> read returned financialGroupId -> use as next anchor.
semantics.name_is_unique_key: Two groups in one list with the same NAME -> HTTP 400 'Group name exists.' Index uniqueness is NOT the constraint; name is.
semantics.display_is_LIFO: Created groups display newest-on-top until reordered (each insert lands at order 0). Use the reorder PUT chain to sort ascending.
semantics.abbreviation_is_truth: accountTypeId + accountTypeClassificationId are integer FKs but UI behavior keys off the accountType abbreviation (A/L/E/R/X). See config/group_account_types.json.
